title: Comanthera angustifolia
intro: Comanthera angustifolia, also known as the 'Red-stemmed Comanthera', is a plant species in the family Eriocaulaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Comanthera angustifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas and infusions, which are often consumed to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Comanthera angustifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. This family is a group of monocots that are commonly known as pipeworts. The Eriocaulaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Comanthera angustifolia is native to Brazil. It is a member of the order Poales.
